mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, compressing equity valuations and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden rise in the unemployment rate above 5.0 percent signals labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 60% of instances when UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand, supporting corporate earnings growth and generating positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60-day returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction that outweighs demand, leading to negative forward returns as earnings expectations are downgraded.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening but SPX returns are positive in > 60% of triggered windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising interest rates combined with falling unemployment creates stagflationary pressure that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, resulting in negative equity returns.
failure_condition: The combination of rising FEDFUNDS and falling UNRATE yields positive 20-day returns in > 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP level below 11500 signals economic stagnation, prompting immediate portfolio rebalancing away from equities and causing negative short-term returns.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns following sub-11500 GDPC1 prints in > 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and consumer spending capacity, driving corporate revenue expansion and positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth consistently fails to predict positive 60-day returns, with Rank IC below 0.02 over rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL index exceeds 210 while the federal funds rate remains below 2.5 percent, real rates turn deeply negative, encouraging equity risk-taking and producing positive forward returns.
failure_condition: The specified inflation-rate regime yields negative or flat SPX returns in > 55% of observed periods.
